column5: evidence

ID `DEF_ADMIN_AGENCY_LOBBYING_TRIGGER` reads ambiguously: could mean "lobbying *by* admin agencies" (actor-axis, parallel to REG_EXECUTIVE_AGENCY) or "lobbying *of* admin agencies" (target-axis, what the description says). Description leads with explicit "TARGET axis" tag; ID does not.
column6: seed example from iter-1; described in handoff brief

column5: ID is explicit: "ELECTED_OFFICIAL_AS_LOBBYIST" pins actor-axis well. But description warns "Distinct from elected officials as the targets of lobbying (which is implicit in any legislative-regime definition)" — a possible-confusion pair the ID itself does not foreclose to a hurried reader. Lower-confidence concern than DEF_ADMIN_AGENCY_LOBBYING_TRIGGER; flagged for cross-domain consistency review.

Same shape as #2. ID is "PUBLIC_EMPLOYEE_AS_LOBBYIST" — actor-leaning. Description disambiguates "as actors", but a hurried reader could conflate with public-employees-as-targets-of-lobbying or with REG_EXECUTIVE_AGENCY (institutional-actor case). Lower-confidence.

Row sits in `domain="registration"` but is a definitional predicate ("Does the law include a definition of 'public entity'?") — the v1.2 D11 test ("Does this answer 'who is a lobbyist?' or 'what does a lobbyist file?'") arguably routes it to `definitions`. D11 framed `definitions` as predicates "on the agent"; "public entity" is precisely such a predicate (organizations as agents). Appears to revisit D11.
column6: confidence: medium; flag for Phase 1 adjudication.

column5: Same as #4 — sub-criterion of the public-entity definition; sits in `registration`. If parent moves to `definitions`, three sub-criteria (`_OWNERSHIP`, `_STRUCTURE`, `_CHARTER`) move with it. Appears to revisit D11.
Same as #5. Appears to revisit D11.
Same as #5. Appears to revisit D11.

The `DEF_*` prefix in this compendium is now strongly associated with `domain="definitions"` (post-v1.2). This row uses `DEF_*` but lives in `registration`. If the row stays in `registration`, the prefix is misleading; if it moves (#4), the prefix becomes consistent. Cross-loaded with C5 above.
Same as #8 (DEF_* prefix vs registration domain).
Same as #8.
Same as #8.

"THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_MATERIALITY_GATE" reads as a quantitative threshold name to a fellow who hasn't read the description. The description leads with "THRESHOLD axis (qualitative)" and explicitly contrasts with `DEF_COMPENSATION_STANDARD` / `DEF_EXPENDITURE_STANDARD` / `DEF_TIME_STANDARD`. Without that disambiguating description, "threshold" reads numeric. (chunk-frame preamble exists for `definitions`.)

ID `THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_PRESENT` does not telegraph that this is the *exemption-framed* counterpart to `DEF_EXPENDITURE_STANDARD` (inclusion-framed). The two rows are deliberately distinct (D11 disambiguation rule), but on ID alone they look like the same row split by accident. Description carries the burden.
Same shape as #13. ID does not encode "exemption-framed" vs `DEF_TIME_STANDARD`'s "inclusion-framed". Description carries the burden.

Sits in `registration`; D11 placed it there because "exemption-framed thresholds are registration gates." But the row is a predicate on whether the agent counts as a lobbyist (the exemption result is "you don't have to file") — same conceptual layer as `DEF_EXPENDITURE_STANDARD`, which D11 placed in `definitions`. Appears to revisit D11; the conceptual line "predicate on the agent ↔ definitions" applies to both rows in the inclusion/exemption pair.
column6: confidence: medium; appears to revisit D11.

column5: Same as #15 — exemption-side time threshold paired with `DEF_TIME_STANDARD` in `definitions`. Appears to revisit D11.
Numeric value of the threshold in #15. If #15 moves, this moves with it. Same conceptual class. Appears to revisit D11.
Same as #17 (numeric value paired with #16). Appears to revisit D11.

Row description is just `Lobbyists.` — bare word lifted from PRI A1 ("Lobbyists."). framework_references span 6 rubrics with substantively different anchorings: PRI A1 (lobbyist-must-register), FOCAL 1.1 (registry coverage of *types* of lobbyists), Newmark 2017/2005/Opheim `def_legislative_lobbying` (legislative lobbying as definition criterion), Hired Guns Q3 ("Is a lobbyist required to file a registration form?"). The single-word description is too thin to disambiguate which question the row is the canonical home for. v2 audit folded `def_legislative_lobbying` here as universal-yes (Decision Log D2); the description does not tell the harness that.

Description is `Volunteer lobbyists.` — same shape as #19. PRI A2 was a single-token criterion; the description inherits PRI's brevity, leaving the harness without scoping (e.g., do volunteer lobbyists for nonprofits qualify? does "volunteer" mean uncompensated only?). Sits across PRI A2 and FOCAL 1.1's broader "types" claim.

Description `Principals who employ a lobbyist.` is bare. framework_references include PRI A3, FOCAL 1.1, OpenSecrets `client_registers` ("Client identifiable in registration data"). OpenSecrets's question is about *whether the client appears in registration data* (data-architecture concern); PRI A3 is about *whether principals must register* (filing requirement). These are subtly different questions; row description picks PRI's framing without acknowledging the OS shading.
Description `Lobbying firms.` — same shape as #19/#20.
Description `Governor's office.` — bare PRI A5.

Description `Executive branch agencies.` — bare PRI A6. The handoff doc explicitly calls out this row as the *actor-axis* counterpart to DEF_ADMIN_AGENCY_LOBBYING_TRIGGER's *target-axis* — i.e., "FDA staff lobbying Congress on behalf of FDA" — but the row description does not encode that distinction.
Description `Legislative branch.` — bare PRI A7.
Description `Independent agencies.` — bare PRI A8.
Description `Local governments.` — bare PRI A9.

Description `Government agencies who lobby other agencies.` is the actor-axis case the chunk-frame preamble for `definitions` cites as out-of-`definitions`-chunk. Row description does not reference DEF_ADMIN_AGENCY_LOBBYING_TRIGGER (target-axis counterpart) for harness disambiguation.
Description `Public entities, other than government agencies.` — bare PRI A11. Suffers same brevity as the rest of the A-series.

ID prefix `EXEMPT_*` distinguishes from `REG_*`/`PARITY_*`, and the field is conceptually an "exemption exists" boolean — but the row name suggests it's the exemption itself, not a question about the law providing one. Description is verbatim PRI B1's question and lands fine; the ID could read as data-about-an-exempt-entity rather than a meta-question.

ID `EXEMPT_GOVT_PARTIAL_RELIEF` suggests partial-relief mechanism existence, but does not pin the axis (is it about "are agencies relieved" or "do agencies get partial vs full relief"?). PRI B2 frames it as a yes/no on relief existing, which the description carries; ID is ambiguous about whether it's a tier-question or a yes/no.

Description is verbatim PRI B3's question — adequate for PRI but row spans only PRI; no description issue. Flag retracted as a C2 — but flag instead under C4 below for cross-row scope clarity vs B1/B2/B4.

PRI B-series (B1 → EXEMPT_GOVT_OFFICIAL_CAPACITY; B2 → EXEMPT_GOVT_PARTIAL_RELIEF; B3 → PARITY_GOVT_AS_LOBBYIST; B4 → PARITY_GOVT_AS_PRINCIPAL) ask four interrelated questions about the same statutory mechanism (government-agency exemption posture). PARITY_GOVT_AS_LOBBYIST and PARITY_GOVT_AS_PRINCIPAL particularly overlap with EXEMPT_GOVT_OFFICIAL_CAPACITY in structure; the harness has no disambiguation rule for which row a given exemption-mechanism statute populates.
Pair to #33; same overlap structure.
framework_references contain only PRI C0 (verbatim "Does the law include a definition of 'public entity'?"). Single rubric, single item — no cluster ambiguity. Flag retracted.

framework_references include PRI D0 ("materiality test (ability to exempt)"), Newmark 2017/2005/Opheim "def_materiality_umbrella" ("standards each imply a materiality test"), Hired Guns Q2_umbrella ("qualification-threshold tiers presume some materiality test"). PRI D0 is **exemption-framed** materiality (qualitative gate to exempt); Newmark/Opheim's "umbrella" framing is *meta* (the existence of standards implies materiality testing) — not a primary statute reading question. CPI Q2_umbrella is similarly meta. The cluster mixes one direct statute-reading question (PRI D0) with three derivative inferences. The derivative refs were added in v1.2's curation-gap fix per D11; v3 should review whether they belong on this row or are better captured as cross-row dedup-map entries.

framework_references include PRI D1_present (statute exempts expenses < $X), FOCAL 1.2 ("no/low financial-or-time threshold"), Hired Guns Q2_expenditure ("expenditure portion of CPI Q2 qualification threshold"). PRI D1_present is exemption-framed; FOCAL 1.2 is a polarity-flipped value-judgment ("no/low threshold is good"); Hired Guns Q2_expenditure is qualification-threshold (inclusion-flavored). Three different framings on one row — D11 nominally resolved this by tightening descriptions ("exemption-framed only"), but the framework_references cluster spans inclusion + exemption.
column6: confidence: medium; appears to revisit D11.

column5: Same shape as #37 — PRI D2_present (exemption) + FOCAL 1.2 (no/low threshold) on a row whose description tightened to "exemption-framed only" per D11.
column6: confidence: medium; appears to revisit D11.

column5: Description `Dollar value of the lobbying-expenditure threshold (whether framed as inclusion or exemption).` claims the row covers both framings, but the *paired* boolean row (THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_PRESENT) was tightened to exemption-only per D11. Inclusion-framed value belongs to DEF_EXPENDITURE_STANDARD's adjacent value (which doesn't exist as a separate row). Asymmetry: PRESENT split into two boolean rows; VALUE remained one numeric row claiming both framings.
column6: confidence: high; appears to revisit D11.

column5: Same asymmetry as #39 — VALUE row claims both framings while PRESENT row was split exemption-only.
column6: confidence: high; appears to revisit D11.

column5: ID `RPT_PRINCIPAL_GATE` suggests a gate-on-the-principal (e.g., a registration gate), but the row is "Principal must file disclosure report" — i.e., the gate is on *whether the principal must file at all*. Description ("Are principals required to disclose?") matches PRI E1a but ID's "GATE" usage isn't anchored anywhere.
ID could read as "lobbyist names that the principal lists" (correct) or "principal-lobbyist-names" as an entity type. Description is clear; ID is ambiguous.
Symmetric pair to #42.

Description `Required component of disclosure report: Other costs such as gifts, entertainment, transportation, and lodging.` is verbatim PRI E1f_iii. Row's framework_references add Newmark 2017/2005's `disc_exp_benefitting_officials` ("Expenditures benefitting public officials or employees") and Opheim's `disc_exp_benefitting_employees` ("Expenditures benefitting public employees including gifts") — substantively narrower than "other costs" (Newmark/Opheim are about *recipient* of the expenditure, not the cost category). Plus FOCAL 7.10 ("Expenditures benefitting public officials or employees..."). The row mixes "cost category" framing (PRI) with "recipient framing" (Newmark/Opheim/FOCAL); description privileges PRI framing.
Symmetric pair to #44. Same mix of cost-category framing (PRI) vs recipient framing (Newmark/Opheim).

framework_references include PRI E1j (major financial contributors), FOCAL 6.2 (sponsors/members), FOCAL 7.3 (income sources + amount), Newmark 2017 `disc_contribs_received` (contributions received from others for lobbying), Opheim `disc_other_influence_peddling` (the catch-all). Five rubric items spanning three substantively different questions: (a) who funds the principal's lobbying (PRI E1j, Newmark, FOCAL 7.3), (b) who funds the principal as a member organization (FOCAL 6.2 sponsors/members), and (c) Opheim's catch-all "other influence peddling" (D6 acknowledged this is a stretch). Cluster is doing too much.

Description `Are lobbyists required to disclose contacts?` is verbatim PRI E2i but is the *gate* row for the contact_log domain (notes field says so explicitly). Description doesn't acknowledge that the answer to this row implies whether any of the 11 CONTACT_* downstream rows can be populated.
Symmetric pair to #47.

RPT_PRINCIPAL_ITEMIZED (PRI E1f_iv: itemized vs lump-sum) is conceptually a binary on whether the principal report is itemized. RPT_EXPENDITURE_FORMAT_GRANULARITY (Sunlight ordinal -1..2: no/lump/broad-categories/itemized-w-dates) is a richer ordinal on the same axis. Both rows can plausibly fire on a "is the report itemized?" statute reading.
Symmetric pair to #49.

Other end of #49/#50 cross-row overlap. RPT_EXPENDITURE_FORMAT_GRANULARITY's description is bare ("Expenditure format granularity.") and offers no rule for how it relates to the side-specific itemized rows.

Description `Expenditure format granularity.` is two words. framework_references span Sunlight (ordinal scale -1..2 with explicit tiers), Newmark 2017/2005 (`disc_categories_expenditures`), Opheim (`disc_spending_by_category`), Hired Guns Q14 ("Are summaries of spending classified by category types?"). Sunlight's ordinal is much richer than Newmark/Opheim's binary "are categories disclosed". Row description doesn't communicate that the data_type is `categorical` (per CSV) and what the ordinal means.

Description `Threshold below which expenditure itemization is not required.` matches Sunlight's concept but Hired Guns Q15 ("What spending must be itemized?") is differently framed — Q15 asks about the rule for itemization scope, not specifically a dollar threshold. Q15 admits answer values that aren't dollar thresholds (e.g., "any expenditure over $X must be itemized" vs "all itemized" vs "no itemization required").

framework_references include PRI E2f_i (lobbyist compensation), FOCAL 7.1 (total lobbying income for consultants/firms), Sunlight `expenditure_transparency` + `compensation`, Newmark 2017/2005 `disc_total_compensation`, Opheim `disc_total_income`, Hired Guns Q13, OpenSecrets `compensation`. The cluster spans: (a) lobbyist *compensation* received (income-side); (b) lobbyist *expenditure on lobbying* via the same line item (expenditure-side, since on a consultant's report, compensation is spending). FOCAL 7.1 is income-side; Sunlight's `expenditure_transparency` is expenditure-side. Cluster bundles two structurally different questions.
Symmetric pair to #54: PRI E1f_i (principal report's compensation paid out) + FOCAL 7.6/7.7 (totals/uncompensated split) + Sunlight + Hired Guns Q27 + OpenSecrets. Same kind of cluster spread.

Description `Required component of disclosure report: Indirect lobbying costs (non-compensation).` is verbatim PRI E2f_ii. Sunlight `expenditure_transparency` (an ordinal) is the only other ref; its mapping to E2f_ii is via the dedup map's disjunction, not a direct equivalence.
Symmetric pair to #56.

Description `Reporting frequency option: Tri-annually (linked with legislative calendar).` is verbatim PRI E1h_iii. Single-rubric row, no cluster; the description is fine on PRI's terms but doesn't disambiguate "tri-annual" (3x/year) from "triennial" (every 3 years) — a known statute-reading hazard.
Symmetric pair to #58.
ID `FREQ_PRINCIPAL_OTHER` doesn't telegraph that this is a free-text catch-all on the cadence ordinal; "OTHER" reads as a category-name. data_type is `free_text` per CSV.
Symmetric pair to #60.

framework_references include Newmark 2017/2005 `def_admin_agency_lobbying` ("Administrative agency lobbying"), Opheim 1991 `def_admin_agency_lobbying` ("Administrative agency lobbying as criterion in definition"), Hired Guns Q1 ("does the definition recognize executive branch lobbyists?"). All four anchor target-axis (lobbying *of* admin/executive agencies). The cluster is cohesive on axis. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.
Cohesive — Newmark/Opheim/2005 `def_elected_officials` actor-axis. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.
Cohesive — Newmark 2017/2005 + Opheim `def_public_employees` actor-axis. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.

framework_references include Newmark 2017/2005 + Opheim `def_compensation_standard` (income-side standard), Hired Guns Q2_compensation ("compensation portion of qualification threshold"). All anchor income-side inclusion threshold. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.
Cohesive — Newmark/Opheim `def_expenditure_standard`. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.
Cohesive — Newmark/Opheim `def_time_standard`. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.

Row sits in `domain="contact_log"` but its substance is "do officials disclose their meeting calendars?" — i.e., it's a *targets* requirement, not a *lobbyist's* contact log. The 11 other CONTACT_* rows in `contact_log` are about lobbyist-side per-meeting fields. RPT_OFFICIAL_DIARY_DISCLOSURE is structurally different — it's about ministerial transparency obligations imposed on officials. Could fit better in a new domain or in `accessibility` (since it tracks portal-published diaries).

ACC_DIARIES_ONLINE (FOCAL 3.2 — "Diaries available online") and RPT_OFFICIAL_DIARY_DISCLOSURE (FOCAL 2.3 — "Ministerial diaries are disclosed monthly") have overlapping subject matter (officials' diaries); ACC version is online-availability, RPT version is disclosure-cadence. Harness rule for distinguishing them is implicit in the domain assignment, not in the descriptions.

framework_references include PRI Q1 (data available in any format/by any channel), FOCAL 3.3 (open license, machine-readable, etc. — much more demanding), Sunlight `document_accessibility` (portal usability ordinal), OpenSecrets `public_lists_baseline`. Cluster spans bare-availability (PRI Q1, easy) to open-data quality (FOCAL 3.3, demanding multi-condition). The description is verbatim PRI Q1, so the cluster has drifted past the description.

framework_references include PRI Q2 (dedicated website), FOCAL 3.1 (lobbyist register is online), Hired Guns Q28+Q29 (online registration *filing* + online spending-report *filing*), OpenSecrets `public_lists`. Cluster mixes "is there a website?" with "do filings happen online?" — different mechanisms. CPI Q28/Q29 specifically are about *filing* online (input side), while PRI Q2/FOCAL 3.1/OS are about *information availability* (output side).

framework_references include PRI Q6 (analysis-ready download), FOCAL 3.3 (open data multi-condition), FOCAL 3.4 (downloadable), Sunlight `document_accessibility`, Hired Guns Q31+Q32 (format of registrations directory + format of spending reports), OpenSecrets `public_download`. FOCAL 3.3 belongs more naturally on ACC_DATA_AVAILABLE_AT_ALL (#70) per the dedup map's disjunctive expression; here it's also referenced for the format dimension. Cluster spans format (CPI Q31/Q32, Sunlight) + downloadability (FOCAL 3.4, OS) + analysis-ready format (PRI Q6) + open-data quality (FOCAL 3.3).

Description `Users can simultaneously sort lobbying data by multiple criteria.` is verbatim PRI Q8. Row name says "(ordinal 0-15)" — but PRI Q8 is the simultaneous-sort score that aggregates the 15 individual sort criteria of PRI Q7a-o. Description does not communicate the ordinal scale.

The 15 ACC_SORT_BY_* rows (PRI Q7a-o) each ask about a single sort dimension; ACC_MULTI_CRITERIA_SORT (PRI Q8) asks about simultaneous-multi-sort capability. The compendium represents Q8's 0-15 ordinal alongside Q7's 15 booleans — but harness rule for whether Q8's score = sum of Q7s, or independent, is not explicit.

Description is verbatim FOCAL 1.3 "The following are included as targets of lobbying: legislative branches, executive branch officials, Ministers, Deputy Ministers..." but US states do not have Ministers / Deputy Ministers / Director-Generals — those are Westminster-system terms. FOCAL was designed for international comparison; the row description carries the international taxonomy untranslated. data_type is `categorical` so the values are state-by-state, but the description implies a particular taxonomy.

Description is verbatim FOCAL 1.4 listing "oral, written, electronic, virtual communications; organising meetings for others; events; phone calls and emails." Same translation issue as #75 — FOCAL list is illustrative, but row description doesn't say so.

framework_references: FOCAL 2.1 ("changes updated close to real time, eg, daily") + Hired Guns Q38 ("How often are lobby lists updated?"). FOCAL 2.1's specific anchor is "daily"; Q38 is open-ended frequency. Cluster cohesive enough; flag is borderline.

Description `Changes (eg, registering/deregistering lobbyists, new clients) are updated close to real time (eg, daily).` is verbatim FOCAL 2.1; states' answer values include "annual" / "manual" / "real-time" — the data_type is `categorical` per CSV. Description does not enumerate the categorical scale.

Description `Ministerial diaries are disclosed monthly (or more frequently).` uses the Westminster term "ministerial." US-state translation is "executive officials' diaries / calendars". Row title says "(calendars / meeting logs)" but description doesn't.
Description `Diaries available online (eg, lobbyists, ministers, ministerial staff).` — same Westminster-term translation issue.

Description `Unique identifiers (eg, for lobbyists, individuals, organisations).` is FOCAL 3.6's text. The row's substance (does the registry maintain stable IDs that persist across filings?) isn't captured by the bare label.

Description `Linked or interconnected data (to other datasets, eg, campaign financing).` is FOCAL 3.7. "Linked data" has a specific technical meaning (RDF / Semantic Web) that FOCAL doesn't necessarily intend; description doesn't disambiguate.
Description `Company registration number.` is FOCAL 4.4. Row title says "(Sec-of-State entity ID or EIN)" — i.e., the implementing concept varies by jurisdiction. Description doesn't say this.

framework_references: FOCAL 4.6 ("Type of lobbyist contract, eg, salaried staff, contracted") + Hired Guns Q10 ("Is a lobbyist required to include on the registration form additional information about the type of lobbying work he or she does?"). Q10 is broader ("type of lobbying work"); FOCAL 4.6 is contract-type specifically. Mismatch.
Description `List of board seats held (eg, in associations, companies).` — bare FOCAL 6.3. Doesn't say *whose* board seats (lobbyist's? principal's? both?).
framework_references: FOCAL 6.4 + Hired Guns Q22 — both ask about lobbyist's business ties to officials. Cohesive. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.

framework_references: FOCAL 7.2 (lobbying income per client) + Newmark 2017/2005 `disc_comp_by_employer` (compensation broken down by employer) + Opheim `disc_sources_of_income` ("Sources of income"). Newmark anchors on *employer/client breakdown* of compensation; Opheim's "sources of income" is a much broader concept (could include grants, member dues, etc.). Cluster mixes per-client breakdown (specific) with sources-of-income (broad).
Row sits in `domain="financial"` but is about *time*, not money. data_type is `numeric`. Could fit better in `reporting` — it's a per-filing numeric field.
Description `Expenditure per issue.` — bare FOCAL 7.8. Doesn't specify granularity (per-bill? per-topic-cluster? aggregate per-issue-area?).

Description `Expenditure on membership/sponsorship of organisations that lobby (eg, trade associations).` is verbatim FOCAL 7.9. Row title narrows to "trade association dues" but description doesn't enforce that narrowing — sponsorship of orgs that lobby is broader than association dues.
framework_references: FOCAL 7.11 + Hired Guns Q24_disclosed (disclosure half of CPI Q24). Both ask about disclosure of campaign/political contributions. Cohesive. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.

ID `CONTACT_BENEFICIARY` reads as "the person/entity who benefited from the contact" — but the row is about "Organisation/interest(s) represented (beneficiary)" — i.e., on whose behalf the lobbyist made the contact. The PRI/FOCAL term "beneficiary" here means "client/principal whose interest is represented", not "official who received a benefit." A reader might confuse this with RPT_HOUSEHOLD_OF_OFFICIAL_SPENDING.
Description `Names of persons contacted and their position/role.` is verbatim FOCAL 8.2. Adequate. **No C2 concern.** Flag retracted.

CONTACT_INSTITUTION (institution/department contacted, FOCAL 8.3) and CONTACT_PERSONS_CONTACTED (persons contacted + position, FOCAL 8.2) overlap when a person is identified by both name and institution; harness rule for whether institution-only contact populates only CONTACT_INSTITUTION or both is implicit.

Description `If a meeting, names of all attendees.` — verbatim FOCAL 8.4. The "if a meeting" qualifier is conditional but description doesn't enumerate non-meeting forms (calls, emails) that wouldn't have an attendees list. data_type is `compound`.

CONTACT_LEGISLATIVE_REFERENCES (FOCAL 8.11, contact_log domain — bill numbers per contact) overlaps with RPT_PRINCIPAL_BILL_SPECIFIC / RPT_LOBBYIST_BILL_SPECIFIC (PRI E1g_ii / E2g_ii — bill-specific reporting on the principal/lobbyist disclosure form). Filing-shape distinct (per-contact vs per-period roll-up) but topically overlapping. Harness rule for which row a state's "list bills lobbied per contact" populates is implicit.

CONTACT_OUTCOMES_SOUGHT (FOCAL 8.10, contact_log — per-contact outcomes/positions) overlaps with RPT_POSITION_TAKEN (Sunlight `position_taken`, reporting domain — positions per filing). Same outcome data at different filing granularities.
framework_references: Sunlight `position_taken` + Opheim `disc_legislation_supported_opposed`. Cohesive on substance. **No C3 concern.** Flag retracted.

Sits in `domain="reporting"` but the substance ("Position taken on each bill or item lobbied") is per-engagement / per-bill data — could fit `contact_log` if the state surfaces it per-meeting. Given Sunlight's framing (per-filing), `reporting` is defensible; flag as low-confidence pending Phase 1 review of domain semantics.

Per the iter-1 surfaced bug + the chunk-frame preamble: the target-axis row DEF_ADMIN_AGENCY_LOBBYING_TRIGGER (definitions domain) and the actor-axis rows REG_GOVT_LOBBYING_GOVT / REG_EXECUTIVE_AGENCY (registration domain) are *intentionally* separate by axis — but neither row's name foregrounds the axis distinction. A harness reading a statute that mentions "executive agency lobbying" without further context could plausibly fire on any of three rows.

DEF_PUBLIC_EMPLOYEE_AS_LOBBYIST (definitions, individual actor) vs REG_GOVT_LOBBYING_GOVT (registration, agency-as-actor). Iter-1's preamble note (in `chunk_frames/definitions.md`) explicitly calls out this disambiguation, but on row IDs alone the boundary isn't communicated.

DEF_COMPENSATION_STANDARD (income-side, inclusion-framed) vs THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_PRESENT (expenditure-side, exemption-framed) — different axis (income vs expenditure) and different framing (inclusion vs exemption). Distinct in design (D11 disambiguation rule) but overlap candidate when a state has a single rule that combines both axes. Low-confidence flag — D11 already addressed.

DEF_EXPENDITURE_STANDARD (inclusion-framed) vs THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_PRESENT (exemption-framed) — explicitly designed as paired rows per D11. Cross-row overlap is a *feature*, not a bug, but the harness rule for when both populate is in the description (D11 addendum), not the row ID.
Same as #103 (paired with THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_TIME_PRESENT).

REG_PRE_LOBBYING_REGISTRATION_WINDOW (CPI Q4: days lobbying allowed before registration required) vs THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_TIME_PRESENT (PRI D2: fraction-of-compensated-time exemption). Both are time-related registration triggers. Decision Log (CPI Q4 entry) explicitly notes "distinct from PRI D2" — but the names alone don't telegraph that distinction.

REG_BILL_SUBJECT_ON_REGISTRATION (CPI Q5: bill/subject on the *registration form*) vs RPT_LOBBYIST_BILL_SPECIFIC (PRI E2g_ii: bill on the *spending report*). Filing-shape distinct (registration vs report), but the harness must distinguish them.

REG_REGISTRATION_RENEWAL_FREQUENCY (CPI Q6: registration cadence) vs FREQ_LOBBYIST_* / FREQ_PRINCIPAL_* (PRI E*h_*: reporting cadence). Both are "frequency" rows; descriptions disambiguate, names overlap.
Row sits in `domain="registration"`. data_type is `boolean` per CSV. Substance is fine for registration. **No concern.** Flag retracted.

RPT_HOUSEHOLD_OF_OFFICIAL_SPENDING (CPI Q21: household of official) overlaps with RPT_PRINCIPAL_OTHER_COSTS / RPT_LOBBYIST_OTHER_COSTS (PRI E*f_iii: gifts/entertainment/transport/lodging) — household is a refinement (recipients of "other costs" extended to household). Statutes that simply say "spending on officials and family members" plausibly populate both.

Description `On the spending report, the lobbyist must identify the specific employer/principal on whose behalf each itemized expenditure was made.` is fine for CPI Q16. **No concern.** Flag retracted.

Description `Lobbyist must identify the legislator (or staff/family member) who received/benefited from each itemized expenditure.` narrows CPI Q17's "recipient of the itemized expenditure" to "legislator (or staff/family member)" — but Q17 is open-ended on recipient identity (could be executive officials, agency staff, etc.). Row title is "(legislator / staff / family)" — same narrowing in title.

Description `Lobbyist who did no spending during a filing period must still file a report indicating no activity.` matches CPI Q25 ("Is a lobbyist who has done no spending..."). Adequate. **No C2 concern.** Flag retracted.

Description `Cost the state agency charges per page for paper copies of disclosure documents (a cost barrier to access).` matches CPI Q33 conceptually, but Q33's response options span more than per-page cost (free / by-page / flat-fee). data_type is `numeric` per CSV; description's "per page" framing presumes the by-page model.

Description `State agency publishes aggregate lobbying spending across all reporting-period deadlines.` matches CPI Q36 ("aggregate spending total by spending-report deadlines"). Ambiguous: is this "publish aggregate by deadline-period" (e.g., quarterly cumulative) or "publish aggregate organized by which deadline produced it"? CPI Q36 is open.

Description `State requires the client/principal to file a registration independently (cleaner data architecture) rather than embedding client identification within the lobbyist's registration.` includes editorial assessment ("cleaner data architecture") that goes beyond what OpenSecrets's binary asks. The OS rubric is value-judgment about data quality; the row description should report-not-rate.

This row asks about filing-architecture separation (client/principal files independently). REG_PRINCIPAL (PRI A3, "Principals who employ a lobbyist must register") asks about whether principals must register at all. A statute that requires principal registration in a *separate* filing would populate both. Harness rule for distinguishing them lives in description, not ID.

RPT_PRINCIPAL_GATE (PRI E1a: principal must file disclosure report) and REG_PRINCIPAL (PRI A3: principal must register) are different filing types but the harness must clearly distinguish "registration" (one-time / annual identification) vs "report" (periodic activity). Row IDs use REG_ vs RPT_ prefixes which encode this — adequate. **No concern.** Flag retracted.

Description `Historical lobbying information is available, downloadable, or at least viewable on the state's website.` is verbatim PRI Q5. The disjunction "available / downloadable / viewable" admits very different fact-patterns; states answer differently depending on which disjunct they meet. data_type is `boolean` (binary), so the disjunction is OR'd into one yes/no. FOCAL 3.8 is also referenced ("downloadable") — narrower than PRI's disjunction.
Description `At least some current lobbying data/information available on the website for the most recent year.` is verbatim PRI Q4. "Some current data" admits low-bar interpretation.

Description `How easily a state's lobbying website can be found.` is verbatim PRI Q3 — but "how easily" is not boolean (data_type is `boolean` per CSV). PRI scored Q3 binary in 2010; data_type matches but description's "how easily" implies degree.

ACC_SORT_BY_PRINCIPAL (Q7a: principal as searchable field) and ACC_SORT_BY_PRINCIPAL_LOCATION (Q7b: principal's location) overlap when a portal lets you search by city/state — "principal address" (Q7a's "city, authority, employer, corporation" includes city) vs "specific address; city" (Q7b). Q7a's parenthetical includes "city" overlapping Q7b. PRI 2010's own rubric had this overlap.
ACC_SORT_BY_LOBBYIST_NAME (Q7c) and ACC_SORT_BY_LOBBYIST_LOCATION (Q7d) — same pattern as #121 but for lobbyist. Q7c's name + Q7d's address are inputs to the same search box on most portals.

ACC_SORT_BY_DATE (Q7e: specific date) vs ACC_SORT_BY_PERIOD (Q7f: specific time period). Date is a point, period is a range; portals usually accept both as date filters. Q7e and Q7f populate together for any portal supporting date-range queries.

ACC_SORT_BY_TOTAL_EXPENDITURES (Q7g) vs ACC_SORT_BY_COMPENSATION (Q7h) vs ACC_SORT_BY_MISC_EXPENSES (Q7i) — three sort-by-money-field rows. Total expenditures = compensation + misc expenses + others; rows are not orthogonal. PRI 2010 priced them as 3 of 15 in Q8's ordinal; v3 should consider whether the 3 rows together = 1 conceptual question (search by money).
ACC_SORT_BY_SECTOR (Q7n) vs ACC_SORT_BY_SUB_SECTOR (Q7o) — sector and sub-sector are nested taxonomies; portals supporting one usually support the other.

ACC_SORT_BY_LEGAL_STATUS (Q7m: govt / non-profit / for-profit) and RPT_PRINCIPAL_BUSINESS_NATURE (PRI E1e: principal nature public/private). Q7m is portal-side filterability of the underlying field that E1e disclosure populates. Pair is correct (different domains, different observability) but worth flagging for cross-domain consistency review.

RPT_SECTOR_DISCLOSED (FOCAL 4.5: sector/sub-sector disclosed in filing) and ACC_SORT_BY_SECTOR / ACC_SORT_BY_SUB_SECTOR (PRI Q7n/Q7o: portal filterability by sector). Cross-domain pair (RPT vs ACC); flag for cross-domain consistency review.

RPT_PRINCIPAL_BUSINESS_NATURE (PRI E1e: principal's business public/private) and the lobbyist-side counterpart RPT_LOBBYIST_PRINCIPAL_NATURE (PRI E2e: lobbyist reports principal's business nature) are symmetric. Description framing is identical but for "lobbyist required to disclose" vs "principal required to disclose".
Symmetric pair to #128.

Description leads with "TARGET axis. Does the state lobbyist definition reach lobbying *directed at* (with administrative or executive-branch agencies as the recipients of lobbying contact) — i.e., is the regime executive-branch-inclusive, or legislative-only?" — combines two questions: (a) does the definition reach admin-agency-lobbying *as a target category* and (b) "is the regime executive-branch-inclusive" (regime-level meta-question). The (b) phrasing risks the harness emitting at regime-meta level rather than at the specific definition-trigger level.

Description `THRESHOLD axis (quantitative, inclusion-framed). Does the statutory definition condition lobbyist status on receiving compensation above a numeric threshold (income-side: paid > $X per period = lobbyist)?` is precise on income-side, inclusion-framed. CPI Q2_compensation framework_reference is "Compensation portion of qualification threshold (CPI Q2)" — but CPI Q2 conflates make/spend ("make/spend $X to qualify"), so Q2 is inherently mixed. The dedup map's `target_expression` handles this with `MERGE` over multiple rows; row description is fine. **No C2 concern.** Flag retracted.

Description includes both inclusion-framed and exemption-framed framings: "Captures both inclusion-framed (...) and exemption-framed (...) phrasings; PRI D0 is exemption-framed, Newmark/Opheim/CPI lean inclusion-framed." This is the qualitative-materiality umbrella row; mixing inclusion and exemption framings on the same row was D11's intent (umbrella), but the harness's iter-1 result shows it correctly populates this row in OH for both regime variants of the qualitative gate. Flag is for Phase 1 to confirm the umbrella framing is intentional and durable.

Description `Are principals required to disclose major financial contributors?` is verbatim PRI E1j but the framework_references stretch the row across FOCAL 6.2/7.3, Newmark `disc_contribs_received`, and Opheim's catch-all `disc_other_influence_peddling`. (See cluster concern #46.) Description should at minimum point to the catch-all D6 fold so the harness knows multiple very-different rubric questions land here.

REG_LOBBYIST (lobbyist must register) vs REG_VOLUNTEER_LOBBYIST (volunteer-lobbyist must register) — REG_VOLUNTEER_LOBBYIST is a special-case modifier on REG_LOBBYIST. A statute that says "all lobbyists, including volunteers, must register" populates both. Harness rule for whether REG_VOLUNTEER_LOBBYIST is *only* about volunteer-specific carve-outs (yes-no on the carve-out existing) or about volunteer-lobbyists' registration in general is implicit.
(Already flagged as #19; this is a re-flag under C4 lens.) Cross-row scope clarity issue compounds the C2 brevity issue.

(Counter to D11 — the row is in `definitions` per D11; flag is ambiguity over whether it should also exist in registration as the target-axis registration trigger.) Lower-confidence; flagging for completeness.

Description `Is there an exemption for government agencies and/or public officials acting within their official capacity?.` is verbatim PRI B1. The trailing period is duplicated (`?.`) — minor formatting issue but signals a copy-from-rubric mechanical pattern across the entire PRI-derived corpus.

**Recurring formatting pattern**: many PRI-derived rows have trailing `?.` (question mark followed by period) in their descriptions (e.g., EXEMPT_GOVT_OFFICIAL_CAPACITY, EXEMPT_GOVT_PARTIAL_RELIEF, PARITY_GOVT_AS_LOBBYIST, PARITY_GOVT_AS_PRINCIPAL, DEF_PUBLIC_ENTITY, RPT_PRINCIPAL_GATE, RPT_PRINCIPAL_CONTACT, RPT_PRINCIPAL_LOBBYIST_NAMES, RPT_PRINCIPAL_LOBBYIST_CONTACT, RPT_PRINCIPAL_BUSINESS_NATURE, RPT_PRINCIPAL_ISSUE_GENERAL, RPT_PRINCIPAL_BILL_SPECIFIC, RPT_PRINCIPAL_CONTACTS_LOGGED, RPT_PRINCIPAL_FINANCIAL_CONTRIBUTORS, RPT_LOBBYIST_GATE, RPT_LOBBYIST_CONTACT, RPT_LOBBYIST_PRINCIPAL_NAMES, RPT_LOBBYIST_PRINCIPAL_CONTACT, RPT_LOBBYIST_PRINCIPAL_NATURE, RPT_LOBBYIST_ISSUE_GENERAL, RPT_LOBBYIST_BILL_SPECIFIC, RPT_LOBBYIST_CONTACTS_LOGGED, ~24 PRI-A/B/E rows). Each is a verbatim PRI question ending in `?` plus a `.` period the loader added. Harmless functionally but indicates `compendium/disclosure_items.csv` was generated by appending `.` to PRI item_text without stripping trailing punctuation. Flag for Phase 1 cosmetic-cleanup batch.

ACC_REGISTRY_UPDATE_FRESHNESS (FOCAL 2.1: registration changes pushed close to real-time) vs ACC_ACTIVITY_DISCLOSURE_FRESHNESS (FOCAL 2.2: activity disclosures pushed close to real-time). Two different freshness measures; descriptions mention they're different but a portal that updates both gets both populated. Reasonable separation; flag is for cross-domain consistency.

RPT_OFFICIAL_DIARY_DISCLOSURE (contact_log) vs ACC_DIARIES_ONLINE (accessibility) vs ACC_ACTIVITY_DISCLOSURE_FRESHNESS (accessibility). Three rows touching diary/calendar disclosure; cross-domain by design but harness rule for which row a state's "diary publication mandate" populates is implicit.

ID `THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_VALUE` doesn't telegraph that this is the dollar number associated with the boolean THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_PRESENT. Pair convention `_PRESENT`/`_VALUE` is internal — not greppable from outside.
Symmetric pair to #141.

framework_references: PRI E2c (names of principals represented), FOCAL 4.1 (full names of lobbyists/orgs), FOCAL 6.1 (client list for consultant lobbyists/firms), FOCAL 7.4 (number of lobbyists employed/contracted), Hired Guns Q9 (identify employer by name on registration). The cluster mixes principal-names (E2c, FOCAL 6.1) with *lobbyist* names (FOCAL 4.1) — they're different parties. FOCAL 4.1 is about lobbyist names specifically; this row is about *the lobbyist's report listing principals*. Cross-loaded reference.

framework_references: PRI E1c (principal lists representing lobbyists), FOCAL 4.1 (full names of lobbyists/orgs), FOCAL 7.4 (number of lobbyists employed/contracted). FOCAL 4.1 ambiguously straddles "lobbyist's name on lobbyist registration" and "lobbyist's name on principal's list" — same as #143.

REG_PRINCIPAL (PRI A3: principals must register) vs REG_LOBBYING_FIRM (PRI A4: lobbying firms must register). When a state's law is about lobbying-firm-as-principal (firm employs lobbyist), both fire.

REG_GOVERNORS_OFFICE (PRI A5) vs REG_EXECUTIVE_AGENCY (PRI A6). The governor's office is structurally part of the executive branch. Hierarchical overlap; PRI 2010 cared about both because some states exempt only the office, not all agencies.

DEF_PUBLIC_ENTITY (PRI C0: does the law include a definition?) and DEF_PUBLIC_ENTITY_OWNERSHIP / _STRUCTURE / _CHARTER (PRI C1/C2/C3: does the definition rely on these criteria?). The C1-C3 rows presuppose C0=Yes; if C0=No, all three sub-rows are `not_applicable` (or `not_addressed` per v1.3 status enum). Harness rule for cascade is implicit.

Sits in `domain="reporting"`. Per FOCAL 6.2 + 7.3 framework_references, this is about funder/sponsor identity. data_type is `boolean`; substance is funding-source identity. Could fit `financial` domain (alongside FIN_INCOME_PER_CLIENT). Flag for Phase 1 review of domain semantics.

The row's framework_references contains only PRI C0; the v2 audit explicitly mentioned that rubrics on definitions may need other refs (e.g., FOCAL 1.1 has "public entities" listed). Coverage gap candidate; defer to Phase 1 (per plan: "If Phase 0 finds rubric items in `papers/text/` that aren't in `framework_dedup_map.csv`, that's a `other-issue` flag with note 'coverage gap, defer to v3+ scope decision'."). FOCAL 1.1 *is* in `framework_dedup_map.csv` mapping to a conjunction of PRI A-series; it's the *concept* of "public entity definition" that may have FOCAL coverage missing on this row.
